signal_title: Code of Practice on Marking and Labelling AI-Generated Content
risk_rationale: The draft bill introduces new requirements for marking and labeling AI-generated content, which is not currently addressed in the TikTok Global Compliance Baseline. This presents a moderate risk as it requires implementation of new features but does not pose an existential threat or require immediate changes.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Misinformation during elections: Prebunking partnerships, fact-check labels
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Providers must mark AI-generated content. Users must label deepfakes and AI-generated texts on public interest matters.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The baseline does not currently require marking and labeling of AI-generated content, which is a new requirement introduced by the EU draft bill.

remediations.title: Implement AI-Generated Content Marking
remediations.description: Develop a system to automatically mark AI-generated content and provide tools for users to label deepfakes and AI-generated texts, especially in public interest matters.
acceptance_criteria: Users can label deepfakes and AI-generated texts
Compliance with EU Code of Practice

business_impact: The implementation of these requirements will enhance transparency and trust among EU users, potentially increasing user engagement and compliance with EU regulations.
recommended_actions: Initiate a project to develop AI content marking features
Engage with EU regulators to ensure alignment with the draft Code of Practice
Monitor the progress of the draft bill to anticipate further changes
